Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Additively manufactured heterogeneous precipitation-strengthened high-entropy alloys with high strength and ductility

Composition_Info.Note: Elemental powders of Co, Ni, Cr, Fe, Al, and Ti were mechanically mixed to the nominal atomic ratio Co47Ni28Cr16Fe3Al3Ti3. The paper states that the overall composition measured by SEM-EDS is close to the designed composition, but the detailed overall values are not provided in the text. Ti-rich segregation was observed in the wall region of the as-printed cellular structure.

Process_Text.original: Elemental powders of Co, Ni, Cr, Fe, Al, and Ti were mechanically mixed via a three-dimensional mixer to the atomic ratio Co47Ni28Cr16Fe3Al3Ti3. During the DED process, a fiber laser with a wavelength of 1080 nm and a spot diameter of 1.2 mm was utilized as the power source. The powder blends were fed into the melt pool on the surface of the substrate via a coaxial powder feeding system under the protection of argon gas. The used powders were heated to 120 °C for 4 h for drying before use. After layer-by-layer deposition, a cuboid part with dimensions of 60 mm (length) × 15 mm (width) × 6 mm (height) was fabricated. The laser power used during DED processing was 750 W, the scanning speed was 480 mm/min, the laser head was moved upward by 0.3 mm after each period of laser scan on the XOY plane, the powder feeding rate was 14.45 g/min, and the hatch distance was 0.8 mm.
Process_Text.simplified: DED of mechanically mixed Co47Ni28Cr16Fe3Al3Ti3 powder using a 1080 nm fiber laser (1.2 mm spot), argon shielding, 750 W power, 480 mm/min scan speed, 0.8 mm hatch spacing, 0.3 mm layer increment, and 14.45 g/min powder feed; powders were dried at 120 °C for 4 h before printing.

Process_Info.Equipment: Fiber laser DED system with coaxial powder feeding system; MTS machine for tensile testing; FEI Scios SEM; JEOL 2100F TEM; CAMECA LEAP 5000 XR APT; diondo d2 micro-CT

Microstructure_Text.original: The as-printed HEA exhibited a typical cellular structure with two separated regions: an interior region and a wall region. Both regions were identified as FCC crystal structures. A Ti-rich zone was present in the wall, and high-density dislocations were observed in the cellular structure. XRD also revealed a single FCC phase constituent. The cellular structure diameter was about 5 μm, and the EBSD-derived grain size was 93 ± 36 μm.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: As-printed cellular structure with FCC interior and wall; Ti-rich segregation in the wall; high-density dislocations; single FCC phase; cell size about 5 μm; EBSD grain size about 93 ± 36 μm.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Room temperature tensile test; loading direction vertical to building direction
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Flat dog-bone specimen, gauge length 12.5 mm, cross-section 3.2 × 1.5 mm²

Composition_Info.Note: Heat-treated state of the same nominal Co47Ni28Cr16Fe3Al3Ti3 alloy. The paper reports region-specific measured compositions in the HT specimen: interior FCC = Co 49.5 ± 1.0, Ni 28.5 ± 0.5, Cr 16.3 ± 0.6, Fe 1.9 ± 0.2, Al 1.7 ± 0.2, Ti 2.1 ± 0.1 at%; wall L12 = Co 20.7 ± 0.7, Ni 53.0 ± 0.8, Cr 2.1 ± 0.2, Fe 0.5 ± 0.1, Al 6.1 ± 0.5, Ti 17.6 ± 0.7 at%; wall FCC = Co 52.2 ± 0.4, Ni 23.7 ± 0.5, Cr 17.3 ± 0.4, Fe 2.7 ± 0.2, Al 1.7 ± 0.1, Ti 2.4 ± 0.1 at%.

Process_Text.original: The same DED-fabricated Co47Ni28Cr16Fe3Al3Ti3 HEA was subsequently isothermally aged at 800 °C for 4 h and then cooled to room temperature in air to incorporate high-density L12 precipitation.
Process_Text.simplified: DED-fabricated Co47Ni28Cr16Fe3Al3Ti3 HEA aged at 800 °C for 4 h, followed by air cooling to room temperature.

Process_Info.Equipment: Fiber laser DED system with coaxial powder feeding system; MTS machine for tensile testing; FEI Scios SEM; JEOL 2100F TEM; CAMECA LEAP 5000 XR APT; diondo d2 micro-CT

Microstructure_Text.original: The heat-treated HEA exhibited an unusual heterogeneous cellular structure. The interior was single-phase FCC, whereas the wall was dual-phase FCC + L12. High-density nanoscale precipitation was found in the wall. Dark-field TEM from the superlattice reflections of the L12 phase demonstrated high-density L12 nanoprecipitation in the wall, with an average precipitate diameter of 31.0 ± 5.1 nm. APT revealed that Ni, Al, and Ti strongly partition into the L12 precipitates, while Co, Cr, and Fe are enriched in the FCC matrix. The volume fraction of the L12 precipitates in the wall was about 0.208.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Heterogeneous cellular structure with single-phase FCC interior and FCC + L12 wall. Wall contains high-density L12 nanoparticles with average size about 31.0 ± 5.1 nm and volume fraction about 20.8%.

Precipitates.Note: Volume fraction in the wall region only.

Composition_Info.Note: Comparison material cited in the introduction from Mu et al. [18]. The paper describes it as a high-entropy alloy with dislocation-precipitate skeleton architectures fabricated by laser PBF (L-PBF).

Process_Text.original: Mu et al. [18] developed an Fe28.0Co29.5Ni27.5Al8.5Ti6.5 HEA with dislocation-precipitate skeleton architectures using laser PBF (L-PBF) technology.
Process_Text.simplified: Reference HEA Fe28.0Co29.5Ni27.5Al8.5Ti6.5 fabricated by L-PBF with dislocation-precipitate skeleton architectures.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: As reported in the cited literature, room-temperature tensile property
Properties_Info.Note: Quoted in the paper as 790 ± 30 MPa.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: As reported in the cited literature, room-temperature tensile property

Composition_Info.Note: Comparison material cited in the introduction from the paper using the same laser PBF technique. The notation is written in the paper as (CoCrNi)94Al3Ti3 at%.

Process_Text.original: Using the same technique, with the introduction of hierarchical structures in terms of grains, cellular substructures, and precipitates in a (CoCrNi)94Al3Ti3 HEA, a high YS of ~836.7 MPa and a large ductility of 27.4 % was obtained.
Process_Text.simplified: Reference (CoCrNi)94Al3Ti3 HEA fabricated by the same laser PBF technique with hierarchical structures.

Microstructure_Text.original: Hierarchical structures in terms of grains, cellular substructures, and precipitates.
